coredata.dc:title: Peroxidase-mimetic carbon dot based nanozyme hydrogel colorimetric sensor for visual trichlorfon detection
coredata.prism:publicationName: Spectrochimica Acta Part A: Molecular and Biomolecular Spectroscopy
coredata.dc:creator: Zhao, Dan
Yan, Zewen
Xiao, Xincai
coredata.dc:description: Organophosphorus pesticide residues pose considerable threats to the environment and public health and have become a global concern. This paper reports the development of a visual sensing platform for the detection of trichlorfon based on a carbon-based nanozyme (abbreviated as Fe-CDs) with peroxidase-mimetic catalytic activity in conjunction with acetylcholinesterase (AChE). The peroxide-mimetic enzyme activity of Fe-CDs can be inhibited by sulfhydryl (–SH) compounds, and AChE can decompose thiocholine (ATCh) to produce –SH-containing thiocholine (TCh), leading to the inability of Fe-CDs to oxidise 3,3′,5,5′-tetramethylbenzidine (TMB) to turn the solution blue, while trichlorfon can inhibit the activity of AChE, thereby recovering the blue colour. This platform achieves sensitive detection of trichlorfon with a linear range of 200–50,000 pM and a detection limit of 157.57 pM and has been successfully applied to the detection of trichlorfon in Chinese cabbage. In addition, encapsulating Fe-CDs, ATCh and TMB in sodium alginate hydrogels and using a smartphone and colour analysis software, a colorimetric hydrogel portable kit is developed, realising the simple and rapid detection of the trichlorfon residue in real samples. This study provides a direct, simple and rapid strategy for the detection of trichlorfon in agricultural products and offers a potential on-site detection tool for food safety monitoring.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Carbon dots based nanozymes
Trichlorfon
Colorimetric sensing platform
Colorimetric hydrogel portable Kit
On-site monitoring

xocs:item-toc-entry.xocs:item-toc-section-title: Detection of trichlorfon in the real samples
Construction of the dual-enzyme cascade colorimetric hydrogel portable kit
Optimisation of the catalytic activity of Fe-CDs
Verifying and optimising the platform’s principle and performance
Detection of trichlorfon in practical samples
Construction of the dual-enzyme cascade colorimetric hydrogel portable kit

xocs:funding-list.xocs:funding-text: This work was funded by the National Natural Science Foundation of China [Grant Number: 21978329 ] and Supported by the Fund for Academic Innovation Teams of South-Central Minzu University [Grant Number: XTZ24027 ].

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: (a) Peroxidase-mimetic activity of Fe-CDs (the concentration of Fe-CDs was fixed at 40 μg/mL). Effects of (b) pH, (c) temperature, (d) TMB concentration, (e) H2O2 concentration and (f) time on the catalytic activity of Fe-CDs.
Verification of the principle behind the detection method of the dual-enzyme cascade colorimetric sensing platform (the concentration of Fe-CDs was fixed at 40 μg/mL).

Effect of (a) reaction time of AChE and ATCh, (b) reaction pH of trichlorfon and AChE, (c) concentration of AChE and (d) concentration of ATCh on the absorbance of the dual-enzyme cascade colorimetric sensing platform.

(a) Impact of trichlorfon concentration on the Ai of the dual-enzyme cascade colorimetric sensing platform is illustrated, with a corresponding photo of the colour changes located in the inset. (b) Correlation curve between lg[trichlorfon] and IE%.

(a) Selectivity and (b) anti-interference capability test of the dual-enzyme cascade colorimetric sensing platform against trichlorfon at 10 nM and interferents at concentrations five times higher (50 nM).

(a) Impact of trichlorfon concentration on the Ai of the dual-enzyme cascade colorimetric sensing platform is illustrated, with a corresponding photo of the colour changes located in the inset. (b) Correlation curve between lg[trichlorfon] and IE% in cabbage juice.
Selectivity and anti-interference capability test of the dual-enzyme cascade colorimetric sensing platform against trichlorfon and interferents at 4 nM in real samples.

(a) Image of anti-interference capability and selectivity test of the dual-enzyme cascade colorimetric hydrogel portable kit. (b) Area (μm2) of channels digitised using ImageJ software. (c) Linear plot of the red (R) channel of the hydrogel portable kit containing trichlorfon (0.02, 0.04, 0.1, 0.2 and 0.5 μM), with true-colour images of the kit taken by a mobile phone.
Schematic diagram of the detection principle for the dual-enzyme cascade colorimetric sensing platform.
Schematic illustration of the colorimetric hydrogel portable kit for trichlorphon detection.
Comparison of performance of different probing strategy of trichlorfon.
Recovery efficiencies of trichlorfon in Chinese cabbage detected by the dual-enzyme cascade colorimetric sensing platform.
Recovery efficiencies of trichlorfon in Chinese cabbage detected by the dual-enzyme cascade colorimetric portable kit.

head.ce:title: Peroxidase-mimetic carbon dot based nanozyme hydrogel colorimetric sensor for visual trichlorfon detection

ce:affiliation.ce:label: a
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: School of Pharmaceutical Sciences, South-Central Minzu University, Wuhan 430074, PR China
ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: School of Pharmaceutical Sciences, South-Central Minzu University, Wuhan 430074, PR China

ce:affiliation.ce:label: b
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: National Demonstration Center for Experimental Ethnopharmacology Education (South-Central Minzu University), Wuhan 430065, PR China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: National Demonstration Center for Experimental Ethnopharmacology Education (South-Central Minzu University)

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: National Demonstration Center for Experimental Ethnopharmacology Education (South-Central Minzu University), Wuhan 430065, PR China

ce:correspondence.ce:text: Corresponding author at: School of Pharmaceutical Sciences, South-Central Minzu University, Wuhan 430074, PR China.

ce:footnote.ce:note-para: Both authors contributed to this article equally.

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: Based on trichlorfon’s impacts upon the adjustment of AChE and TCh to the enzyme activity of Fe-CDs, and introduce the resulting color change of the platform into hydrogel, a visual detection of trichlorfon has been established.

Organophosphorus pesticide residues pose considerable threats to the environment and public health and have become a global concern. This paper reports the development of a visual sensing platform for the detection of trichlorfon based on a carbon-based nanozyme (abbreviated as Fe-CDs) with peroxidase-mimetic catalytic activity in conjunction with acetylcholinesterase (AChE). The peroxide-mimetic enzyme activity of Fe-CDs can be inhibited by sulfhydryl (–SH) compounds, and AChE can decompose thiocholine (ATCh) to produce –SH-containing thiocholine (TCh), leading to the inability of Fe-CDs to oxidise 3,3′,5,5′-tetramethylbenzidine (TMB) to turn the solution blue, while trichlorfon can inhibit the activity of AChE, thereby recovering the blue colour. This platform achieves sensitive detection of trichlorfon with a linear range of 200–50,000 pM and a detection limit of 157.57 pM and has been successfully applied to the detection of trichlorfon in Chinese cabbage. In addition, encapsulating Fe-CDs, ATCh and TMB in sodium alginate hydrogels and using a smartphone and colour analysis software, a colorimetric hydrogel portable kit is developed, realising the simple and rapid detection of the trichlorfon residue in real samples. This study provides a direct, simple and rapid strategy for the detection of trichlorfon in agricultural products and offers a potential on-site detection tool for food safety monitoring.

ce:data-availability.ce:para: Data will be made available on request.

ce:section.ce:para: Pesticides are crucial in the agricultural and food sectors, effectively controlling pests, enhancing crop yields and ensuring the quality and quantity of agricultural products [1]. They are key to global agriculture modernisation [2]. Organophosphates are the major components in herbicides and insecticides [3] and have been widely used in agriculture and gardening. Trichlorfon, highly praised for its excellent effectiveness and diverse applications, has raised global concerns due to its residue problems in agricultural products and food. Exposure to organophosphorus pesticides (OPs) poses significant health risks and is associated with serious diseases, such as cancer, birth defects and infertility. Long-term exposure may even cause severe damage to the nervous system [4–8]. Therefore, the development of rapid, sensitive and simple strategies for the detection of trichlorfon is crucial for food safety and public health.

Although several highly sensitive and selective detection methods of trichlorfon have been developed, such as near-infrared spectroscopy [9], point turbid extraction coupled with high-performance liquid chromatography-ultraviolet detection [10], molecularly imprinted techniques [11,12] and magnetic nanomaterial analysis [13], these strategies still exhibit obvious drawbacks such as high cost, time-consuming and considerable dependence on equipment and expert personnel, limiting their application in on-site detection. By contrast, colorimetric methods have attracted considerable attention because of their merits such as rapidity, low cost and easy operation [14] and can be enhanced by portable equipment, low-cost equipment and artificial intelligence–based image recognition techniques [15]. In particular, enzyme-based colorimetric methods provide the possibility of highly sensitive and selective detection of trichlorfon and have become the focus of present studies.

Enzyme-based colorimetric methods realise visual detection based on the highly catalytic effect of enzymes to convert the target substance into a coloured product to achieve signal amplification and visualisation [16]. This method overcomes the drawbacks of traditional colorimetric methods based on colour developers or dyes, such as insufficient sensitivity and susceptibility to interferences; thus, it exhibits obvious merits in sensitivity and selectivity in detection. However, the high difficulty associated with biological purification and hard storage conditions greatly limit its applications. Fortunately, the emergence of nanozymes has successfully resolved these issues.

Currently, most reported nanozymes are based on metals, which are typically composed of metal ions such as gold, silver, iron and copper, and exhibit enzyme-like properties. For instance, Xiao et al. [17] proposed a colorimetric sensor based on the TCPP functional CuCo2O4 nanocomposite and realised that sensitive detection through OPs could inhibit AChE activity. Li and his team [18] realised sensitive detection of OPs based on Cd/CoOOH composite materials. The AChE activity enhanced the quenched fluorescence of the material caused by Forster resonance energy transfer, and the OPs specifically blocked the AChE activity and caused the quenching of the fluorescence. These developments indicate that nanozyme colorimetric methods have become an effective tool in OP detection. Although the catalytic effects of these metal nanozymes are highly effective, their drawbacks of high cost, poor stability, biotoxicity, environmental risks, health concerns and pharmacokinetic problems are still great challenges to their practical applications.

Carbon-based nanozymes have emerged as safer alternatives to hazardous metal-based nanozymes. Carbon-based nanozymes exhibit stable biocatalytic properties under extreme pH and temperature environments and possess merits such as high stability and good biocompatibility, environmental-friendliness, low cost and ease for mass production [19]. Carbon dot nanozymes, in particular, have attracted interest from researchers due to their super-small particle size, uniform dispersibility in the aqueous phase and easy-to-modify structure [20]. For example, Zhang et al. [21] developed a novel metal-free carbon quantum dot nanozyme that simultaneously exhibits GOX and HRP activity and realised the biomimetic detection of glucose. Sun and his team [22] developed a novel nitrogen-doped carbon dot nanozyme (P-NCDs), which can produce monoclinic oxygen under UV light, and realised the highly sensitive colorimetric detection of Hg2+. Carbon dot nanozymes overcome the drawbacks of metal-based nanozymes to some extent and thus possess great potential in the fields of biosensing, medical diagnosis and disease treatment.

In response to the public demand for the detection of trichlorfon, particularly, people in remote areas, this research loads the nanozyme into the hydrogel to realise on-site visual detection of trichlorfon, greatly expanding the practical value of this colorimetric platform. Sodium alginate (SA) hydrogel provides a great immobilised phase for constructing a portable colorimetric platform for the dual-enzyme cascade platform due to its low cost and simple gel formation process [23], high transparency, high flexibility [24,25], excellent hydrophilicity [26,27] and broad internal 3D network structure [23]. Li et al. [28] immobilised a gold nanocluster/MnO2 composite into a hydrogel and monitored paraoxon residues in Chinese cabbage through image analysis using smartphone apps. This hydrogel-based nanozyme colorimetric strategy enabled on-site rapid screening and large-scale monitoring, enhanced the efficiency and reliability of detection for food and agricultural products and provided effective technique support for food safety and environmental protection. It helped promote sustainable agricultural development and environmental protection, laying the foundation for the next generation of intelligent pesticide detection technology.

This paper reports the construction of a dual-enzyme (Fe-CDs and AChE) cascade colorimetric sensing platform for the trace-level analysis of trichlorfon. In the presence of hydrogen peroxide, Fe-CDs catalyses the oxidation of TMB into blue oxidised TMB (ox-TMB). Thiolates, however, can inhibit this oxidation process. AChE can produce TCh through hydrolysis of ATCh, and TCh can reduce ox-TMB back to TMB, causing the disappearance of the solution’s blue colour. However, the addition of trichlorfon inhibits the activity of AChE, turning the solution blue. This platform enables the quantitative detection of trichlorfon through the inhibition and recovery of absorbance of CDs and exhibits merits such as rapidness, sensitivity and broad detection range. Enriched with surface groups containing oxygen or nitrogen, such as hydroxyl and amino groups, the Fe-CDs can form hydrogen bonds with water molecules. Coupled with the catalytic properties of Fe-CDs and the simplicity of the colorimetric method, this platform can be integrated into hydrogel soft materials. Visual and portable detection of the trichlorfon residue in real samples is achieved through optimised loading and distribution of nanozymes in hydrogel.

Trichlorfon, Glyphosate, Fipronil, Acetamiprid, ATCh, and TMB were procured from Sigma-Aldrich Reagent Co., Ltd. Thiacloprid was obtained from Beijing Bailewei Technology Co., Ltd. Omethoate was purchased from AccuStandard, Inc·H2O2, N-[3-(trimethoxysilyl)propyl]ethylenediamine (DAMO) and SA were acquired from Aladdin (Shanghai) Co., Ltd. AChE was purchased from Shanghai Yuanye Biotechnology Co., Ltd. Calcium chloride (CaCl2), Lactose, Saccharose, Thiourea, Catechol, Ferric chloride hexahydratev(FeCl3·6H2O), Methanol (MeOH), and Dimethyl sulfoxide (DMSO) were obtained from China National Medicines Group Chemical Reagent Co., Ltd. L-Cystine and L-Leucine were purchased from Biosharp Life Sciences Co., Ltd. The reagents were of analytical grade and was used without further purification. All the aforementioned reagents and the phosphate-buffered saline (PBS, 20 mM) are of analytical grade and did not require further purification prior to use.

The green luminescent Fe-CDs were synthesized via a one-step hydrothermal method using 1.8 mg of thiourea, 2.2 mg of catechol, 0.1 mL of DAMO, and 29 mg of FeCl3·6H2O as raw materials, under a reaction temperature of 200 ℃ for 3 h. When the reaction was completed, the acquired brown solution was first concentrated by centrifugation at 8000 rpm for 10 min to remove insoluble precipitates, then concentrated by centrifugation at 4000 rpm in an ultrafiltration tube (MWCO = 3000) for 5 min, and finally freeze-dried to obtain brown solids.

In the reaction system, 60 μL of AChE (80 mU/mL) and 20 μL of trichlorfon (at varying concentrations) were added. After a 20-minute incubation at 37 °C, 100 μL of PBS(pH = 7.5) and 40 μL of ATCh (1 mM) were introduced, followed by an additional hour of incubation. Subsequently, 670 μL of PBS (pH 2.8), 50 μL of TMB (10 mM), and 30 μL of H2O2 (0.4 mM) were added. The mixture was allowed to develop color for a duration of 2 h until a stable color was achieved. The absorbance of the samples at the maximum wavelength λmax = 652 nm in the UV–Vis spectrum was then recorded, denoted as Ai . Additionally, two control experiments should be conducted. The absorbance was recorded as A0 when trichlorfon and AChE were replaced with pure water. The absorbance was recorded as A when trichlorfon was replaced with pure water. All other procedures are consistent with the aforementioned method. The dual-enzyme cascade colorimetric sensing platform for the detection of trichlorfon has been successfully constructed.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Detection of trichlorfon in the real samples
ce:section.ce:para: Chinese cabbage was chosen to validate the practical application of the dual-enzyme cascade colorimetric sensing platform. The potential of Fe-CDs for detecting trichlorfon in real samples was evaluated using the spike recovery method. Initially, 6 g of thoroughly washed Chinese cabbage leaves were frozen at −80 °C, grounded into a homogenate using a mortar and pestle post-freezing, and then mixed with 30 mL of deionized water. The mixture was centrifuged at 10,000 rpm for 10 min, and the supernatant was allowed to stand at 4 °C for 1 day. Subsequently, the supernatant was diluted tenfold to obtain the Chinese cabbage sample solution. This solution was used to prepare a series of trichlorfon solutions with gradient concentrations. The dual-enzyme cascade colorimetric sensing platform was employed to perform linearity testing and repeatability experiments on the sample solutions, record the Ai and calculate the IE%. Scatter plots were generated, and linear regression was conducted to calculate the relative standard deviation values and recovery rates.

When evaluating the interference of actual samples on the dual-enzyme cascade colorimetric sensing platform, in addition to Chinese cabbage, we expanded our research scope to include four other common vegetables: sweet corn, white radish, cucumber, and lettuce. To ensure experimental consistency and comparability, the processing method for these vegetables was the same as that for Chinese cabbage. Subsequently, the processed vegetable sample solutions were mixed with trichlorfon to prepare a 4 nM trichlorfon solution sample, simulating the actual situation of trichlorfon residue and calculate the IE%. Through this method, we were able to assess the adaptability and anti-interference capability of the sensing platform for different vegetable matrices in detecting trichlorfon.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Construction of the dual-enzyme cascade colorimetric hydrogel portable kit
ce:section.ce:para: Dissolved SA solution (5 mL, 18 mg/mL, PBS of pH = 7.5) containing ATCh (100 μL, 10 mM),TMB (50 μL, 10 mM) and Fe-CDs (300 μL, 2 mg/mL), introduced it into the cap of a 1.5 mL Eppendorf tube. The hydrogel was obtained by adding 100 μL of CaCl2 solution (0.02 g/mL) to the SA solution. The obtained portable kit can be stored at 4℃ for further use. Added different concentrations of trichlorfon (10 μL), AChE (80 mU/mL, 30 μL), and PBS (pH = 7.5, 20 mM, 50 μL) to the tube of the portable kit, reacted at 37 °C for 20 min, then inverted the kit to incubate the reaction mixture with ATCh in 37°C for 1 h. Then added PBS (pH = 2.8, 20 mM, 400 μL) to regulate the pH of the system and H2O2 (400 mM, 30 μL) to further catalyze TMB by Fe-CDs for 2 h at room temperature. Capture the color of the hydrogel under filament lamp using mobile phone, and split the color channel into RGB channels by ImageJ software.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Optimisation of the catalytic activity of Fe-CDs
ce:section.ce:para: The catalytic activity of prepared Fe-CDs was first investigated using TMB as the colorimetric substrate and Fe-CDs as the mimetic enzyme. As shown in Fig. 1 a, without Fe-CDs, the mixture of TMB and H2O2 could only produce a weak absorption peak at 652 nm; with Fe-CDs, the absorption was considerably enhanced, illustrating its ability to catalyse the oxidisation process of TMB into ox-TMB. By contrast, in the absence of H2O2, the peak at 652 nm almost disappeared, indicating that Fe-CDs alone could not effectively oxidise TMB. These experimental results confirmed the peroxidase-mimetic activity of the prepared Fe-CDs.

To enhance the catalysis efficiency of the mimetic peroxidase activity of Fe-CDs, several experiment parameters of catalysis conditions were optimized, including pH value of PBS, reaction temperature, concentration of TMB and H2O2, and reaction time, to examine the impacts of these factors upon the response signals. The concentration of Fe-CDs was set to 40 μg/mL, and the reaction time was set to 30 min, except for the experiments where the reaction time was varied. The enhancement of pH value of PBS led to stronger response signal in the absorbance at 652 nm (Abs652), and further enhancement of pH value caused dramatical drop of absorption (Fig. 1b), indicating that pH = 3.5 was the optimal condition for catalysis process. As for reaction temperature, the catalytic activity of Fe-CDs stayed stable under the temperature 20 °C to 28°C, while a heated condition (temperature above 40°C) resulted in weakened catalysis ability (Fig. 1c). To simplify the operation, room temperature was set as the standard temperature for catalysis reaction. Subsequently, under the condition where the concentration of H2O2 was fixed at 12 mM, the concentration of TMB was adjusted (90 to 800 μM). The results in Fig. 1d indicated that at a TMB concentration of 500 μM, there was no significant increase in the Abs652, hence this concentration was selected for subsequent experiments. When the TMB concentration was fixed at 500 μM, the concentration of H2O2 was altered within the range of 1.5 to 48 mM. The findings demonstrated that at a hydrogen peroxide concentration of 12 mM, Abs652 started to stay stable, and the concentrations were thus set as standard concentration for the following experiments (Fig. 1e). The impact of reaction time was also critical to the system, and was thus investigated by fixing Fe-CDs at 40 μg/mL and adjusting reaction time (10, 30, 40, 50, 60, 90, 120, 150, 180 min). As show in Fig. 1f, the absorbance enhanced with the increase of reaction time, and stayed stable at 120 min, which was then set as optimal reaction time. Through these optimization process, the optimal condition for the best properties of Fe-CDs’ application in sensing platform was determined, and then the impacts of the additions of AChE, ATCh and trichlorfon were studied.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Verifying and optimising the platform’s principle and performance
ce:section.ce:para: As shown in Fig. 2 , without AChE or ATCh, the Fe-CDs/TMB/H2O2 system exhibited the strongest absorption, close to 0.35 with the appearance of the blue signal. The presence of AChE, ATCh or Ca2+ alone did not affect the intensity of the absorption peaks (Fig. S1) because the mere existence of either AChE or ATCh could not affect the production of ox-TMB. However, the presence of both AChE and ATCh produced reductive TCh, which could inhibit the catalytic ability of Fe-CDs and caused a dramatic drop in peak intensity to zero, leading to the disappearance of the blue colour in the solution. The addition of ATCh after the interaction between trichlorfon and acetylcholinesterase led to the recovery of absorption back to 0.3. This occurred because trichlorfon inhibits acetylcholinesterase activity, reducing the production of TCh, which in turn increases the amount of TMB oxidised into ox-TMB, resulting in the appearance of a blue colour.

These experimental results demonstrate the possibility of detecting trichlorfon based on the colour change of the solution based on the interaction between trichlorfon and AChE/Fe-CDs dual-enzyme cascade system. Therefore, a dual-enzyme cascade colorimetric sensing platform for the detection of trichlorfon is proposed in Scheme 1 . The reaction parameters, such as the concentrations of AChE and ATCh, were optimised to enhance the sensitivity and reliability of the platform in practical application scenarios.

The difference in absorbances at 652 nm with and without AChE (ΔAbs652) was chosen as the response signal of the system. With increasing reaction time, the amount of TCh produced by the interaction between ATCh and AChE increased, reducing the amount of ox-TMB produced by the oxidisation of TMB catalysed by Fe-CDS. The response signal became stable after 60 min (Fig. 3 a). The AChE enzyme activity was greatly inhibited at pH 4, and with an increase in pH value, the concentration of TCh first increased and then started to decrease at pH 7.5 (Fig. 3b).

The activity of AChE was then accurately determined by fixing the concentration of ATCh. As shown in Fig. 3c, the response signal increased with increasing AChE activity, which was attributed to the production of more TCh. The signal remained stable at an AChE concentration reaching 4.8 mU/mL. Then, making the AChE activity stable and increasing ATCh concentration from 20 to 80 μM, as shown in Fig. 3d, the increased ATCh concentration enhanced the response signal, which remained stable at 40 μM. The optimal reaction time of trichlorfon’s inhibiting AChE activity was then investigated through the variable controlling method, where the concentration of trichlorfon was set constant (40 μM) and the interaction time between trichlorfon and AChE was adjusted. Fig. S2 shows that the presence of trichlorfon inhibited the activity of AChE immediately and reached equilibrium at 20 min. The optimal incubation time of the system was thus set at 20 min.

The concentration of the standard trichlorfon solution was determined by established dual-enzyme cascade colorimetric sensing under optimal conditions. With inhibition efficiency (IE%) as the response signal, IE% is expressed as follows:
I E % =
A i
- A /
A 0
- A
where A0 is the absorbance without AChE and trichlorfon, A is the absorbance with AChE but without trichlorfon and Ai is the absorbance with both trichlorfon and AChE [17]. As shown in Fig. 4 a, with increasing trichlorfon concentration, AChE activity was inhibited, leading to the decreased concentration of TCh. Correspondingly, the increased concentration of ox-TMB caused the enhancement of absorbance. The inset of Fig. 4a shows the colour change of the solution with the increase in trichlorfon.

Fig. 4b shows the linear fitting curve to the scatter plot with lg[trichlorfon] as the horizontal coordinate and IE% as the vertical coordinate over a mass concentration range of 200–20,000 pM. The results show that IE% increased with increasing trichlorfon concentration and these two variables exhibited a strong linear correlation. With the fitted calibration curve as IE% = −0.7539 + 0.3636 lg[trichlorfon] (200–20,000 pM), the linear correlation coefficient was 0.9962 and the limit of detection (LOD) was 138.25 pM, better than those of other reported detection strategies (Table 1 ). Therefore, the proposed colorimetric detection platform possesses broad linear response range and high sensitivity.

Specificity is a pivotal attribute for the practical utility of analytical methods. A series of pesticides were used to assess the specificity of the established colorimetric detection platform, such as thiacloprid, glyphosate, acetamiprid and fipronil. Even their concentrations reached five times that of trichlorfon (10 nM), and their impacts on the absorbance of the platform were almost negligible (Fig. 5 a), exhibiting excellent selectivity of the platform to trichlorfon. As shown in Fig. 5b, the response of the platform to the presence of trichlorfon remained the same regardless of the addition of interference substances (lactose, sucrose, l-leucine, l-cysteine, Ca2+ and K+), underscoring the platform’s reliable ability to discriminate trichlorfon effectively amidst potential interferences. This enhanced selectivity can be attributed to the accurate enzymatic recognition of AChE and the inorganic interaction between Fe-CDs and TCh produced by enzymatic reaction.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Detection of trichlorfon in practical samples
ce:section.ce:para: To prove the practicability and reliability of the established dual-enzyme colorimetric platform, the standard addition method was used to determine trichlorfon in Chinese cabbage. The Chinese cabbage was homogenised, centrifuged and then diluted 10 times to measure its absorbance to eliminate the impact of background noise caused by its inherent colour. Trichlorfon solutions were added at different concentrations to obtain a series of spiked solutions with concentration gradients (200–50,000 pM). The spiked solutions were added to the established colorimetric sensing platform and Abs652 was recorded.

As shown in Fig. 6 a, the absorbance of the system increased with increasing trichlorfon concentration and then plateaued, which might be the result of complete saturation of the enzyme by trichlorfon. IE% was calculated using the acquired data, and the scatter plot was plotted (Fig. 6b). The fitted calibration curve was IE% = −0.6069 + 0.2874 lg[trichlorfon] (200–50,000 pM), with a linear correlation coefficient of 0.9995 and an LOD of 157.57 pM.

This curve can be used to monitor the unknown concentration of trichlorfon in practical samples and can avoid the impact of food substrates on the sensing system. Moreover, the recovery rate of trichlorfon was determined using the standard spiking method, and the experiments were repeated three times at each concentration (Fig. S3).The recovery rate and relative standard deviation (RSD) are shown in Table 2 . Given these excellent properties, the system can be effectively used to determine trichlorfon residues in Chinese cabbage, providing a valuable tool for assessing food safety.

Two other OPs (trichlorfon and omethoate) and one non-OP (thiamethoxam) were selected to study the selectivity of the established colorimetric system. Four common vegetables (fruit corn, turnip, cucumber and lettuce) were then chosen as substrates besides Chinese cabbage because they are usually eaten raw, increasing the potential risk of exposing these pesticides into the food chain. The processing method for the four vegetables was the same as that for Chinese cabbage. In addition, the concentration of trichlorfon in the prepared juices of the five types of vegetables was 4 nM.

As shown in Fig. 7 , for the five vegetable samples, the presence of thiamethoxam did not cause obvious changes in the absorbance of the sensing system, while trichlorfon caused an obvious enhancement in absorption at the same concentration (4 nM). However, the OP omethoate exhibited a different response signal in the system. The experimental results showed the high anti-interference capability of the established colorimetric sensing platform against another OP (i.e. thiamethoxam) and effectively distinguished trichlorfon from omethoate at the same concentration level, highlighting its great potential for monitoring trichlorfon residues in various vegetables.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Construction of the dual-enzyme cascade colorimetric hydrogel portable kit
ce:section.ce:para: Polysaccharide-based hydrogels, such as SA hydrogels, have greatly reduced dependence on equipment for content determination due to their high transparency and flexibility, direct demonstration of colour change and excellent compatibility with a wide range of assay platforms, finally providing a direct and visual interpretation of results with the assistance of smartphones. The 3D networks formed by the SA polymer provide sufficient space for CD loading and ensure a fast reaction rate inside the hydrogel. Meanwhile, the hydrophilic nature of the hydrogel environment facilitates the even dispersion of CDs [32]. This characteristic effectively prevents the aggregation of these nanomaterials [33] and shields them from interference by macromolecules present in the external environment, leading to stability, anti-interference ability and expanded applications in on-site scenarios. In addition, the low cost of raw materials for SA-based hydrogels and the simple gel-forming step at room temperature by introducing calcium ions can greatly reduce the cost and difficulty of kit preparation. A batch of hydrogels for the visual detection of trichlorfon was developed by embedding Fe-CDs and ATCh into the SA hydrogel matrix. The specific detection process is illustrated in Scheme 2 .

The molecular diffusion rate is closely related to the hydrogel porosity, which is controlled by the concentration of the hydrogel precursor [34]. When the concentration of SA hydrogel precursor reached 18 mg/mL, the blue colour reaction of the Fe-CDs hydrogel system reached equilibrium at 120 min after the addition of TMB and H2O2; however, at a concentration of 24 mg/mL, the reaction became slower and stabilised at 160 min due to slower molecular diffusion. Therefore, a concentration of 18 mg/mL was selected to prepare the colorimetric hydrogel portable kit to obtain an optimal molecular diffusion rate. Due to the mechanical stability and flexibility of SA hydrogels, hydrogels loaded with Fe-CDs, TMB and ATCh were moulded onto the lids of 1.5 mL centrifuge tubes. By adjusting the volume of the added gel solution, the thickness of the kits could be controlled. The experimental results showed that the addition of 240 μL of SA solution to each well achieved a uniform colour change in the gel; however, excessive thickness led to the colour change of the surface rather than the entire gel. The analytical performance of the kit was evaluated using AChE solutions at concentrations of 8–40 mU/mL (Fig. S4). Given that AChE is the most expensive part of this sensing platform, a concentration of 20 mU/mL was chosen for trichlorfon detection to ensure cost-effectiveness, and this concentration can ensure the sufficient signal output and optimal performance of the system.

To evaluate the anti-interference ability and practicability of the prepared hydrogel portable kit, with Chinese cabbage as the practical sample, solutions of trichlorfon, omethoate and thiamethoxam in equal concentrations (2 μM) were prepared from the Chinese cabbage juice. The three pesticide solutions were added to the kit for 20 min to inhibit AChE activity and then placed upside down to ensure that the mixture absorbed into the hydrogel of the kit. After setting the temperature at 37 ℃ for reaction for 1 h, H2O2 solutions were added into the tube, which was then placed upside down for 2 h to ensure full contact for the reaction and the appearance of the blue signal. As shown in Fig. 8 a, thiamethoxam did not cause the appearance of the blue colour in the hydrogel. By contrast, the omethoate solution induced a blue colour. Among the three solutions, the trichlorfon solution led to the most pronounced colour change of the hydrogel, demonstrating the system’s ability to distinguish them at identical concentrations.

Linear assays were conducted for the trichlorfon solutions of different concentrations (20, 40, 100, 200 and 500 nM) dissolved in Chinese cabbage juice, and the operation steps were the same as above. To avoid inaccuracy caused by the naked-eye observation, smartphones were used to take pictures of the kit under an LED lamp and the relative positions of the kit, smartphone and light were fixed to minimise the influence of the environment. The recorded true-colour images were decomposed into RGB channels and converted into digital data in pixels (μm2) by ImageJ, and the RBG values refer to the response signals of trichlorfon concentrations. Compared with blue (B) and green (G) channels, the red (R) channel exhibits stronger signal response (Fig. 8b). As shown in the inset of Fig. 8c, with increasing concentration of trichlorfon from 20 to 500 nM, the blue colour enhancement of the hydrogel was observed. Through the image-processing algorithm, the R channel signal of the kit increases with increasing trichlorfon concentration, with the fitted linear equation of y = − 228.0 + 55.04x in the range of 20–500 nM (R2 = 0.9959). Moreover, standard spiking recovery assays were performed in three parallel experiments for each concentration to determine kit recovery rates (Fig. S5). The recovery rate and RSD are shown in Table 3 . The results show that the prepared kits enabled accurate monitoring of trichlorfon using smartphone and relevant software. This portable platform considerably reduces detection costs by using a reusable master mould, simple operation and minimal test reagents.

This study reports the development of a dual-enzyme colorimetric sensing platform based on Fe-CDs that exhibited an excellent ability to detect trichlorfon in Chinese cabbage. Fe-CDs play a dual role in this system: as a nanocatalyst for TMB and as a recogniser for TCh. Through the synergistic effect of Fe-CDs and AChE, the platform achieved highly sensitive detection of trichlorfon with a wide detection range of 200–50,000 pM (R2 = 0.9995) and an LOD of 157.57 pM. The recovery rate of the platform was in the range of 93.89 %–102.12 % with an RSD of less than 1.14 %, exhibiting good accuracy and repeatability in detection. Herein, a portable colorimetric kit based on SA hydrogel provided a porous diffusion path for small molecules and effectively isolated interferences from the external environment. The smartphone and image-processing software were introduced to convert colour changes into digital data for more accurate detection, enabling on-time quantitative monitoring of the practical samples with a detection range of 20–500 nM (R2 = 0.9959). The recovery rate was 94.48 % with an RSD of 1.51 %. Therefore, this study demonstrates the considerable potential of the hydrogel kit in quantitative detection of pesticides for applications in food safety and risk assessment, which enhances the convenience and efficiency of detection, bringing about a breakthrough in food safety monitoring technology.
Dan Zhao: Writing – original draft, Writing – review & editing. Zewen Yan: Formal analysis, Investigation, Data curation. Xincai Xiao: Methodology, Funding acquisition, Conceptualization, Validation.

This work was funded by the National Natural Science Foundation of China [Grant Number: 21978329] and Supported by the Fund for Academic Innovation Teams of South-Central Minzu University [Grant Number: XTZ24027].

ce:conflict-of-interest.ce:para: The authors declare that they have no known competing financial interests or personal relationships that could have appeared to influence the work reported in this paper.

ce:section.ce:label: Appendix A
ce:section.ce:para: Supplementary data to this article can be found online at https://doi.org/10.1016/j.saa.2025.126027.
